articles.title: Energy agency expects global oil demand to fall in 2026
articles.summary: Energy agency expects global oil demand to fall in 2026 Global oil demand will fall by about one million barrels a day in 2026 as the war between the United States and Iran weighs on the world economy, the International Energy Agency has said. The agency warned that the fighting was creating fresh uncertainty for consumers, businesses and energy markets. Higher risks around shipping and supply could weaken demand further if the conflict escalates. Oil production rose by 4.1 million barrels a day in June as some supplies returned to the market. articles.title: Thai sailors sue shipping firm after Strait of Hormuz attack
articles.summary: Thai sailors sue shipping firm after Strait of Hormuz attack Three Thai sailors have sued their former employer after a projectile struck their cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz in March, killing three crew members. Panithi Tumkaew, Noppadon Wongsuvan and Surades Manpuen accuse Precious Shipping, two affiliates and the vessel’s captain of endangering their lives and dismissing them before their nine-month contracts ended. articles.summary: Israeli forces beat Palestinian family and fire tear gas into home Several members of a Palestinian family were wounded after Israeli forces raided their home in al-Mughayyir, northeast of Ramallah in the occupied West Bank, on Friday, Wafa reported. Israeli soldiers beat members of Muhammad Hamed Abu Aliya’s family and fired tear gas canisters inside the house, causing injuries from the assault and gas inhalation, security sources told Wafa. articles.summary: Tankers transit Hormuz despite renewed US-Iran tensions Vessels transporting liquified natural gas (LNG) have resumed passage through the Strait of Hormuz in recent days, according to ship-tracking data, with authorities in Tokyo reporting that 22 Japan-linked ships have left the Gulf since Tuesday. Despite renewed fighting between the US and Iran, data from Kpler and LSEG has showed at least five LNG tankers entering the strait, including from China, Greece and Qatar. articles.summary: Israel told US of alleged Iranian plot to kill Trump Israel provided the US with new intelligence that indicated an Iranian plot to kill US President Donald Trump, according to the Wall Street Journal. articles.summary: Israeli strikes, demolitions continue in Lebanon State media reported that an Israeli drone targeted a truck early on Friday in Nabatieh, southern Lebanon, resulting in the wounding of two people.  According to the National News Agency, the attack occurred while the truck was dumping waste on the outskirts of the towns of Choukin and Kfardjal. Meanwhile, demolition operations continued in southern Lebanon, with multiple blasts heard in Nabatieh's Khiam.

articles.title: Report: Hamas spokesperson survives Israeli air strike
articles.summary: Report: Hamas spokesperson survives Israeli air strike Hamas spokesperson Hazem Qassem reportedly survived an Israeli attack that occurred on Thursday west of Gaza City, according to Al Arabiya television. Based on a security source in the territory, one of the Israeli air strikes targeted the Hamas official's car. However, Qassem was not in the car at the time of the attack. His body guard was killed instead.  On Thursday, at least six people were killed by Israeli shelling across the blockaded enclave.
